title: Bring a goal. We'll bring the machine.
seoDescription: See the Genius Monkey platform live, your goals, your channels, your reporting. A real expert walks you through it, built around what you're trying to do.

sections.draftLabel: Request a Demo page, V2 (braver register, V1 untouched)
sections.notes: The form is a placeholder; its field set is a UX and reviewer decision.

content.text: A real expert walks you through the platform, the dashboard, and the reporting, built around whatever you're trying to do. Canned decks are for vendors with something to hide.

content.note: Demo request form. Content requirement: plain field labels, as few fields as possible. Required field set to be confirmed by the reviewer.

items: The dashboard, live. Your spend and placements the way clients see them every day.
The reporting. Attribution that shows which ad moved which number, first impression to conversion.
Your channels. What a campaign for your goal looks like across the screens your buyers use.

content.text: A goal, a budget range, and any reporting you've stopped trusting. That's plenty. Acronyms are our department.
Someone who runs campaigns for a living and can answer the hard question in the room. If programmatic is the wrong tool for your goal, they'll say so out loud. Cheaper for everyone.

Country Thunder turned every ad dollar into $230. onsemi's cost per conversion fell from $2,000 to under a buck. BrickHouse Nutrition cut acquisition costs by two thirds in three months. Thirty minutes against numbers like those is a good trade.

items: V2 terms. Braver H1 ("Bring a goal. We'll bring the machine.", picks up the machine motif from platform-v2's approved H1). Committed register, zero "X, not Y" constructions by deliberate self-ban. Conversion page stays deliberately short, same as V1 (~230 words of page copy).
"What happens after you submit" still out, skipped per the reviewer 2026-07-06.

Register commitments to react to: "Canned decks are for vendors with something to hide." (sharper than V1's "Not a canned deck", pokes competitors harder), "Acronyms are our department.", "they'll say so out loud. Cheaper for everyone." (the honesty promise, recommitted), "under a buck", "The human" as a section header.
"Thirty minutes" remains the visitor's time investment only, no demo-length promise. "Two thirds in three months" is $83 to under $28, cleared math on the cleared fact.
Facts: Country Thunder ($230 per $1), onsemi ($2,000 to under $1, no timeframe attached), BrickHouse ($83 to under $28 in three months). All in brand/facts.md. No response-time or demo-length claims.
Form fields deliberately not drafted, same as V1.
